title: cephalocereus schlumbergeri
intro: Cephalocereus schlumbergeri, also known as the "Queen of the Andes," is a species of cactus native to the Andes mountains in South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Cephalocereus schlumbergeri are said to include reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from its stems or roots, which can be dried and used to make teas, infusions, or extracts for medicinal purposes.
